label: near_miss

case_target.description: 当前 case 的主控制对象是 ProxyService.isSafeUrl()，它负责执行协议、域名与解析后地址范围校验。

should_not_report.reason: ProxyService.isSafeUrl() validates the URL against the modeled SSRF policy before HttpUtil.getUrl() is called. The guard enforces https-only scheme, domain allowlist, and IP-range checking on allowlisted hosts. HttpUtil.getUrl() only ever receives pre-validated URLs from the service layer. The RestTemplate in HttpUtil is configured with setInstanceFollowRedirects(false) as defense-in-depth.
should_not_report.false_positive_risk: A system that inspects util/HttpUtil.java in isolation will see REST_TEMPLATE.getForObject(url, ...) with an externally supplied String argument and flag it as an unguarded SSRF sink. A system that traces the call from the controller but fails to enter isSafeUrl() inside fetchRemote() will reach HttpUtil.getUrl() via the service and still incorrectly flag getForObject() at util/HttpUtil.java line 26.

controls.description: Scheme allowlist: only 'https' is permitted, blocking http://, file://, gopher://, dict:// and other dangerous schemes.
Domain allowlist: only api.example.com and cdn.example.com are permitted, blocking arbitrary external or internal hostnames.

IP-range defense-in-depth: after the domain allowlist passes, InetAddress.getByName() resolves the allowlisted hostname and rejects loopback, site-local, and link-local results (127.x, 10.x, 192.168.x, 169.254.x etc.). DNS resolution failure is silently ignored and treated as passing — DNS rebinding and resolution failures are not modeled as security boundaries here.
setInstanceFollowRedirects(false) prevents the HTTP client from following 3xx redirects that could redirect to an internal host after passing the initial URL validation in the service layer.

notes: Difficulty is 'hard': guard and sink are in different layers/files. HttpUtil is an intentionally low-level HTTP helper that trusts callers to validate URLs. setInstanceFollowRedirects(false) is in the util layer as defense-in-depth — testing whether the system recognises cross-layer cooperative defense.
